title: toxicodendron quercifolium
intro: Toxicodendron quercifolium, also known as the Rhus quercifolia or yellow sapsucker, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of urushiol, a toxic compound. The health benefits of Toxicodendron quercifolium include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, and its herbal preparations can be found in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, although its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional due to its toxic nature.
